title: salicornia procumbens subsp. strictissima
intro: Salicornia procumbens subsp. strictissima, also known as beach greens or marsh samphire, is a type of succulent plant that grows in coastal areas and is a member of the glasswort family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Salicornia procumbens subsp. strictissima include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and capsules, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
